goal: Land the §6.3 quality-maximalist response-assembly + bonus-context-selector pair as a 2-feature coordinated bundle in `crates/ucil-core/`. F09 extends `context_compiler.rs` with a pure-deterministic `assemble_response` projection that applies session dedup + relevance threshold + token annotation over a `CrossGroupFusedOutcome`; F11 introduces a NEW `bonus_selector.rs` module with a UCIL-owned `BonusContextSource` trait (DEC-0008 §4 dependency-inversion seam) + `select_bonus_context` attachment helper for the 8 master-plan §1.3 bonus categories (conventions, pitfalls, quality_issues, related_code, tests_to_update, blast_radius, history, security). Both features unblock by WO-0087 (`RepoMap` / `RepoMap` ranking surface) + WO-0068 (`CrossGroupFusedHit` / `CrossGroupFusedOutcome`); the bundle shape follows the SEXTUPLE-validated bundle-coordinated-features template (WO-0067 / WO-0068 / WO-0070 / WO-0083 / WO-0084 / WO-0085) — F11 depends on F09 directly per feature-list, the two share §6.3 line 660-690 master-plan contract verbatim, and §6.3 line 670 explicitly chains response-assembly into bonus-context attachment as one mental model.
plan_summary: Two-feature coordinated bundle around master-plan §6.3. F09 extends the existing `crates/ucil-core/src/context_compiler.rs` (the WO-0087 module that already houses `RepoMap` + `personalized_page_rank`) with: (a) public `AssembledResponse { hits: Vec<CrossGroupFusedHit>, meta: ResponseMeta }` + `ResponseMeta { token_count: usize, deduped_count: usize, filtered_count: usize, total_input: usize }`; (b) public `ResponseAssemblyOptions { relevance_threshold: f64 = 0.1, dedup_enabled: bool = true }` (Default); (c) public `assemble_response(outcome: &CrossGroupFusedOutcome, files_in_context: &HashSet<PathBuf>, options: &ResponseAssemblyOptions) -> AssembledResponse` entry point; (d) public `hit_token_estimate(&CrossGroupFusedHit) -> usize` heuristic mirroring the WO-0087 `entity_token_estimate` 4-char-per-token shape (signature.len()/4 + snippet.len()/4 + 8 overhead constant); (e) frozen module-root test `test_response_assembly` (DEC-0007 substring selector resolution `context_compiler::test_response_assembly`). F11 lands a NEW `crates/ucil-core/src/bonus_selector.rs` module with: (a) `pub trait BonusContextSource { fn fetch_bonus(&self, hit: &CrossGroupFusedHit) -> BonusEntries; }` (DEC-0008 §4 dependency-inversion seam — no IO/network/subprocess in the trait surface); (b) `pub struct BonusEntries { conventions: Vec<String>, pitfalls: Vec<String>, quality_issues: Vec<String>, related_code: Vec<String>, tests_to_update: Vec<String>, blast_radius: Vec<String>, history: Vec<String>, security: Vec<String> }` (8 categories per master-plan §1.3 lines 65-79); (c) `pub struct HitWithBonus { hit: CrossGroupFusedHit, bonus: Option<BonusEntries> }` with `bonus = Some(_)` IFF score ≥ attachment_threshold; (d) `pub struct BonusSelectionOptions { attachment_threshold: f64 = 0.1 }` (Default — same threshold as F09 per master-plan §6.3 line 667-668); (e) `pub fn select_bonus_context<S: BonusContextSource>(hits: &[CrossGroupFusedHit], source: &S, options: &BonusSelectionOptions) -> Vec<HitWithBonus>`; (f) frozen module-root test `test_bonus_context_selection` with a `TestBonusContextSource` `#[cfg(test)]` impl (per DEC-0008 §4 + WO-0083 `TestG4Source` precedent + WO-0085 `TestG7Source` precedent). lib.rs gets `pub mod bonus_selector;` + `pub use bonus_selector::{...};` + extension of the existing `pub use context_compiler::{...};` re-export block. Mutation contract: M1 (F09 — bypass session dedup), M2 (F09 — bypass relevance threshold via `f64::NEG_INFINITY`), M3 (F11 — invert attachment threshold direction). All three reversible via `git checkout --` + md5sum verify. Estimated 5-7 commits per DEC-0005 module-coherence.
scope_in: 1. **Bundle-coordinated-features template SEXTUPLE-validated** (WO-0067 / WO-0068 / WO-0070 / WO-0083 / WO-0084 / WO-0085 §planner). Two cohesive features around shared master-plan §6.3 line 660-690 contract (response-assembly + bonus-context-selector chain — §6.3 line 670 explicitly: 'Append relevant bonus context: conventions that apply, pitfalls for this code, quality issues in these files, tests that cover this code, blast radius if editing — skip bonus categories with zero relevant entries'). F11 depends on F09 (per feature-list deps) and they share the §6.3 thematic invariant; bundling avoids cross-feature merge-base churn. Cite WO-0086 §planner explicit emission recommendation: 'response-assembly bundle (P3-W10-F09 + F11 + F13 — response shape + bonus-context-selector + warm processors)' — note: F13 (warm processors) is in `crates/ucil-daemon/` (different crate, different runtime concern: background scheduler vs synchronous response shape) and is NOT included in this WO; emit F13 as a separate WO-0089 candidate.

2. **F09 file location**: extend EXISTING `crates/ucil-core/src/context_compiler.rs` (the WO-0087 module that already houses `RepoMap` + `personalized_page_rank`). F09's acceptance test selector per feature-list is `cargo test -p ucil-core context_compiler::test_response_assembly` — the test name + module name pin the file. NO new module file for F09; ADDITIVE extension of the existing 862-LOC file (post-WO ≈ 1100-1300 LOC).

3. **F11 file location**: create NEW `crates/ucil-core/src/bonus_selector.rs` (file does NOT exist at planner-time — pre-flight `! test -e crates/ucil-core/src/bonus_selector.rs` returns true; verified `missing-good` at planner-time). F11's acceptance test selector per feature-list is `cargo test -p ucil-core bonus_selector::test_bonus_context_selection` — the module name pins the file path.

4. **F09 public types** (each with full rustdoc citing master-plan §6.3 + a `# Examples` block per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Docs):
    (a) `pub struct ResponseMeta { pub token_count: usize, pub deduped_count: usize, pub filtered_count: usize, pub total_input: usize }`. `total_input` = input outcome's hit count BEFORE any filtering; `deduped_count` = number of hits removed because their `file_path` was in `files_in_context`; `filtered_count` = number of hits removed because their `fused_score < relevance_threshold`; `token_count` = `Σ hit_token_estimate(h) for h in surviving hits`. Invariant: `token_count + deduped_tokens + filtered_tokens >= 0` (token-count NEVER negative). Invariant: `total_input == surviving + deduped_count + filtered_count`. NO non_exhaustive — this is a stable response-shape contract per master-plan §6.3 line 700-701 (`_meta.token_count` for host-adapter trimming).
    (b) `pub struct AssembledResponse { pub hits: Vec<CrossGroupFusedHit>, pub meta: ResponseMeta }`. `hits` is sorted descending by `fused_score` (preserving the input outcome's order — this WO does NOT re-sort; it ONLY filters). `meta` carries the four counters from (a). Invariant: `hits.len() == meta.total_input - meta.deduped_count - meta.filtered_count`.
    (c) `pub struct ResponseAssemblyOptions { pub relevance_threshold: f64, pub dedup_enabled: bool }` with `Default` impl returning `relevance_threshold: 0.1, dedup_enabled: true` (the master-plan §6.3 line 667 + line 663 defaults; documented inline). NO `#[non_exhaustive]` on the options struct — adding new fields is permitted via additive non-breaking changes per scope_in #15.
    NO error enum needed for F09 — it cannot fail (pure-deterministic projection over already-validated `CrossGroupFusedOutcome` input; empty input produces empty output, never panics).

5. **F09 entry point** (with `# Examples` rustdoc per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Docs):
    `pub fn assemble_response(outcome: &CrossGroupFusedOutcome, files_in_context: &HashSet<PathBuf>, options: &ResponseAssemblyOptions) -> AssembledResponse`. Pure-deterministic; never panics; never returns `Result`. Algorithm per master-plan §6.3 lines 665-685:
    Step 1 — Capture `total_input = outcome.hits.len()`.
    Step 2 — Apply session dedup (if `options.dedup_enabled`): partition hits into `(kept, deduped)` where a hit is `deduped` IFF `files_in_context.contains(&hit.file_path)`. NEVER use string-suffix matching or PathBuf-canonicalization — `HashSet::contains` over `PathBuf` is the one invariant comparison. If `dedup_enabled == false`, skip this step (kept = all input).
    Step 3 — Apply relevance threshold: partition kept into `(surviving, filtered)` where a hit is `filtered` IFF `hit.fused_score < options.relevance_threshold`. Use `<` STRICT comparison (a hit with `fused_score == relevance_threshold` IS surviving — boundary inclusion per master-plan §6.3 line 667 'score < 0.1' verbatim).
    Step 4 — Compute `token_count = surviving.iter().map(|h| hit_token_estimate(h)).sum::<usize>()`.
    Step 5 — Construct + return `AssembledResponse { hits: surviving, meta: ResponseMeta { token_count, deduped_count, filtered_count, total_input } }`.
    NO sorting (the input `CrossGroupFusedOutcome.hits` is already sorted descending per WO-0068 §6.2 contract; F09 preserves the order).

6. **F09 token-estimate helper**:
    `pub fn hit_token_estimate(hit: &CrossGroupFusedHit) -> usize`. Returns `(hit.snippet.chars().count() + hit.file_path.to_string_lossy().chars().count()) / 4 + 8`. The `+ 8` constant is the per-hit Markdown-rendering overhead (file-path header + line-range annotation + delimiters per master-plan §6.3 line 700 'Format: Markdown with file paths as headers, full code blocks'). The `/ 4` is the cl100k_base lower-bound (1 token ≈ 4 chars for ASCII source — same heuristic as WO-0087 `entity_token_estimate`). MUST use `chars().count()` not `len()` (the latter returns bytes, biasing toward overcounts on multi-byte source — per WO-0087 scope_in #6 carry). Document inline as parallel to `entity_token_estimate` with a doc-comment cross-reference (`See also: [`entity_token_estimate`]`).

7. **F09 frozen test** `test_response_assembly` MUST live at MODULE ROOT of `context_compiler.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests { ... }`) per DEC-0007 + WO-0067/0068/0070/0083/0084/0085 §verifier precedent. Write as `#[cfg(test)] #[test] fn test_response_assembly() { ... }`. The selector `cargo test -p ucil-core context_compiler::test_response_assembly` MUST resolve uniquely without `--exact` (substring match — the `tests::` infix would break this). Pre-flight: `cargo test -p ucil-core context_compiler::test_response_assembly --list 2>&1 | grep -c 'test_response_assembly: test'` returns exactly 1.

8. **F09 test body** — construct a `CrossGroupFusedOutcome` with 5 hits with known fused_scores [0.50 (file_a.rs), 0.30 (file_b.rs), 0.05 (file_c.rs), 0.00 (file_d.rs), 0.20 (file_a.rs again)]. Construct `files_in_context = HashSet::from([PathBuf::from("file_a.rs")])`. Construct `options = ResponseAssemblyOptions::default()` (threshold 0.1, dedup_enabled true). Call `assemble_response(&outcome, &files_in_context, &options)` and assert (each with SA-tagged operator-readable panic body per WO-0085 §verifier `(SAn) <semantic name>; left: ..., right: ...` pattern):
    (SA1) `result.meta.total_input == 5` (input count preserved). Panic body: `(SA1) total_input expected 5; observed {n}`.
    (SA2) `result.meta.deduped_count == 2` (file_a.rs appears twice — both hits are deduped). Panic body: `(SA2) deduped_count expected 2; observed {n}`. **This assertion is targeted by M1 (skip session dedup).**
    (SA3) `result.meta.filtered_count == 2` (score 0.05 + 0.00 are below 0.1 threshold). Panic body: `(SA3) filtered_count expected 2; observed {n}`. **This assertion is targeted by M2 (bypass relevance threshold).**
    (SA4) `result.hits.len() == 1` (only file_b.rs at 0.30 survives both filters; 5 - 2 dedup - 2 filter = 1). Panic body: `(SA4) surviving hits expected 1; observed {n}`.
    (SA5) `result.hits[0].file_path == PathBuf::from("file_b.rs")` (the surviving hit IS file_b.rs). Panic body: `(SA5) survivor file_path expected file_b.rs; observed {fp:?}`.
    (SA6) `result.meta.token_count > 0 && result.meta.token_count < 1000` (sanity — a single hit with a short snippet gives ~10-50 tokens; the `<1000` upper bound is a backstop against estimator-runaway regressions). Panic body: `(SA6) token_count out of plausible range [1, 1000]; observed {n}`.
    (SA7) `result.meta.total_input == result.hits.len() + result.meta.deduped_count + result.meta.filtered_count` (the conservation invariant from #4(b)). Panic body: `(SA7) total_input != hits + deduped + filtered: {a} vs {b} + {c} + {d}`.
    (SA8) **Boundary inclusion at threshold 0.1**: construct a SECOND outcome with one hit at exactly `fused_score = 0.1`, dedup-empty input; assert `result.hits.len() == 1` (boundary-inclusion: `0.1 < 0.1` is false so the hit IS surviving). Panic body: `(SA8) boundary-inclusion at threshold: hit at score=0.1 expected to survive; observed filtered_count={n}`. Use a sub-block like `{ let outcome2 = ...; let result2 = assemble_response(...); assert!(result2.hits.len() == 1, "(SA8) boundary-inclusion at threshold: ..."); }` to keep the assertion ordered AFTER SA1-SA7.
    Test MUST run synchronously (no `#[tokio::test]`); MUST NOT touch the network; uses only `std::collections::HashSet` + `std::path::PathBuf`; NO `tempfile` / `tokio` / `rusqlite` deps for this test.

9. **F11 public types** (each with full rustdoc citing master-plan §1.3 + §6.3 + a `# Examples` block where non-trivial — per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Docs):
    (a) `pub struct BonusEntries { pub conventions: Vec<String>, pub pitfalls: Vec<String>, pub quality_issues: Vec<String>, pub related_code: Vec<String>, pub tests_to_update: Vec<String>, pub blast_radius: Vec<String>, pub history: Vec<String>, pub security: Vec<String> }` (the 8 categories per master-plan §1.3 lines 65-79). Default impl returns all-empty vectors. Each field's rustdoc cites the master-plan line where the category is first named (§1.3 lines 65-79 + §6.3 line 670-672). NO `#[non_exhaustive]` — these are the named master-plan categories; future additions require an ADR.
    (b) `pub struct HitWithBonus { pub hit: CrossGroupFusedHit, pub bonus: Option<BonusEntries> }`. Invariant: `bonus.is_some()` IFF the hit's `fused_score >= options.attachment_threshold` at the call to `select_bonus_context`. The `Option<>` makes the boundary explicit at the type level — below-threshold hits carry NO bonus payload at all (NOT an empty `BonusEntries`).
    (c) `pub struct BonusSelectionOptions { pub attachment_threshold: f64 }` with `Default` impl returning `attachment_threshold: 0.1` (the master-plan §6.3 line 667-668 default; same threshold as F09's `relevance_threshold` — the two thresholds are intentionally aligned). NO `#[non_exhaustive]` — additive extension via new fields with Default impls is permitted per scope_in #15.
    NO error enum for F11 — `select_bonus_context` is pure-deterministic projection (the `BonusContextSource::fetch_bonus` call may panic in a malformed test impl, but production impls in a future WO will return `Result` per their own crate's error contract).

11. **F11 entry point** (with `# Examples` rustdoc):
    `pub fn select_bonus_context<S: BonusContextSource>(hits: &[CrossGroupFusedHit], source: &S, options: &BonusSelectionOptions) -> Vec<HitWithBonus>`. Generic over `S` (NOT `dyn` — per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Crate-layout the default is monomorphisation; `dyn` only when storing heterogeneous impls). Pure-deterministic; never panics; algorithm:
    For each hit `h` in `hits`:
      - If `h.fused_score >= options.attachment_threshold`: `bonus = Some(source.fetch_bonus(h))`.
      - Else: `bonus = None`.
      - Push `HitWithBonus { hit: h.clone(), bonus }` to the output Vec.
    Use `>=` NON-STRICT comparison (a hit with `fused_score == attachment_threshold` GETS bonus attached — boundary inclusion per master-plan §6.3 line 667-668 'when relevance score ≥0.1' VERBATIM, which is the inverse boundary of F09's strict `<` filter; F09's threshold-survivors === F11's bonus-recipients exactly when `relevance_threshold == attachment_threshold`).

13. **F11 frozen test** `test_bonus_context_selection` MUST live at MODULE ROOT of `bonus_selector.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests { ... }`) per DEC-0007 + bundle-coordinated-features template precedent. Pre-flight: `cargo test -p ucil-core bonus_selector::test_bonus_context_selection --list 2>&1 | grep -c 'test_bonus_context_selection: test'` returns exactly 1.

14. **F11 test body** — construct a vec of 4 `CrossGroupFusedHit` with fused_scores [0.50, 0.10, 0.05, 0.00] (one above threshold strictly, one AT threshold, two below). Construct `TestBonusContextSource` with canned `BonusEntries { conventions: vec!["snake_case".to_string()], pitfalls: vec!["avoid mut".to_string()], quality_issues: vec!["warning_X".to_string()], related_code: vec!["foo.rs:10".to_string()], tests_to_update: vec!["test_foo".to_string()], blast_radius: vec!["bar.rs".to_string()], history: vec!["PR #42".to_string()], security: vec!["CVE-1234".to_string()] }`. Call `select_bonus_context(&hits, &source, &BonusSelectionOptions::default())` and assert:
    (SA1) `result.len() == 4` (all hits surface in output, regardless of whether bonus is attached). Panic body: `(SA1) result count expected 4; observed {n}`.
    (SA2) `result[0].bonus.is_some() && result[0].bonus.as_ref().unwrap().conventions == vec!["snake_case"]` (top hit at 0.50 GETS bonus, all 8 categories populated). Panic body: `(SA2) top-hit bonus expected Some with conventions=["snake_case"]; observed {b:?}`.
    (SA3) `result[1].bonus.is_some()` (boundary at 0.10 INCLUDED — `0.10 >= 0.10` is true). Panic body: `(SA3) boundary-hit (score=0.10) bonus expected Some; observed {b:?}`. **This assertion is targeted by M3 (invert attachment threshold direction).**
    (SA4) `result[2].bonus.is_none()` (below-threshold hit at 0.05 DOES NOT get bonus). Panic body: `(SA4) below-threshold hit (score=0.05) bonus expected None; observed {b:?}`. **This assertion is targeted by M3 (invert attachment threshold direction).**
    (SA5) `result[3].bonus.is_none()` (zero-score hit DOES NOT get bonus). Panic body: `(SA5) zero-score hit bonus expected None; observed {b:?}`.
    (SA6) **All-8-categories sentinel canary**: `let bonus_top = result[0].bonus.as_ref().unwrap();` then assert ALL 8 fields are non-empty AND match the canned content (`conventions == vec!["snake_case"]`, `pitfalls == vec!["avoid mut"]`, ..., `security == vec!["CVE-1234"]`). Panic body per category: `(SA6.<cat>) bonus.<cat> expected <expected>; observed {actual:?}`. This is the §1.3 canonical-8-categories regression canary — analogous to the WO-0067 §6.2 sentinel-row + WO-0085 §5.7 severity sentinel-row patterns.
    (SA7) `let pos_zero = result[3].hit.fused_score; assert!(pos_zero == 0.0)` (hit identity preserved through projection). Panic body: `(SA7) hit identity preserved: fused_score expected 0.0; observed {n}`.
    Test MUST run synchronously (no `#[tokio::test]`); MUST NOT touch the network; uses only `std::path::PathBuf` + the local `TestBonusContextSource` impl.

15. **lib.rs updates**:
    (a) Add `pub mod bonus_selector;` in alphabetical position (between `pub mod ceqp;` and `pub mod context_compiler;` IF `bonus_selector` < `ceqp` in alphabetical sort — yes, 'b' < 'c'; place `pub mod bonus_selector;` BEFORE `pub mod ceqp;`). Verify via `grep -B2 -A2 '^pub mod' crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs` after the edit shows the alphabetical block: `pub mod bonus_selector; pub mod ceqp; pub mod context_compiler; pub mod cross_group; pub mod fusion; pub mod incremental; pub mod knowledge_graph; pub mod otel; pub mod schema_migration; pub mod tier_merger; pub mod types;`.
    (b) Add `pub use bonus_selector::{BonusContextSource, BonusEntries, BonusSelectionOptions, HitWithBonus, select_bonus_context};` as a NEW re-export block. Place it BEFORE the existing `pub use context_compiler::{...};` block (alphabetical `bonus_selector` < `context_compiler`). The block uses `#[rustfmt::skip]` to keep the entire `pub use` line on one source line — same shape as the existing `pub use cross_group::{...};` block at lib.rs:42 — so the AC03 grep `grep -qE '^pub use bonus_selector::\{' crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs` matches without depending on rustfmt's wrapping heuristic.
    (c) Extend the EXISTING `pub use context_compiler::{...};` block at lib.rs:36 to ADD the new F09-public types: `AssembledResponse, ResponseAssemblyOptions, ResponseMeta, assemble_response, hit_token_estimate`. The post-edit line is: `pub use context_compiler::{AssembledResponse, RankedSymbol, RepoMap, RepoMapError, RepoMapOptions, ResponseAssemblyOptions, ResponseMeta, assemble_response, hit_token_estimate};` (alphabetical sort of all 9 names). Use `#[rustfmt::skip]` to keep it single-line per the existing pattern.
    NO logic in `lib.rs`. Pre-flight: post-edit `grep -E '^(pub mod|pub use) ' crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs` MUST show `pub mod bonus_selector;` exactly once AND `pub use bonus_selector::\{...\};` exactly once.

16. **Pre-bake 3 mutation entries** — pattern carried from WO-0042 → WO-0087 (now 43+ WOs deep). Each mutation: in-place `Edit` patch + targeted cargo-test selector + `git checkout --` restore + md5sum verify.
    **M1 (F09 — bypass session dedup)**: Edit `crates/ucil-core/src/context_compiler.rs` to bypass the session-dedup partition. Find the line containing `files_in_context.contains(&hit.file_path)` (or the `Step 2` partition expression in `assemble_response`) and change the closure body to `false` (always claim the file is NOT in context, so nothing is deduped). Re-run `cargo test -p ucil-core context_compiler::test_response_assembly`; MUST FAIL with the SA2 panic (`(SA2) deduped_count expected 2; observed 0`) AND/OR SA4/SA7 panics. Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-core/src/context_compiler.rs`.
    **M2 (F09 — bypass relevance threshold via `f64::NEG_INFINITY`)** — applies the WO-0086 §planner constant-flip preference: change `options.relevance_threshold` to `f64::NEG_INFINITY` AT THE COMPARISON SITE only (NOT the struct field default). The patch substitutes the literal source line `if hit.fused_score < options.relevance_threshold` with `if hit.fused_score < f64::NEG_INFINITY` — every hit's `fused_score` is finite-positive so `< NEG_INFINITY` is always false, and nothing is filtered. Re-run; MUST FAIL with the SA3 panic (`(SA3) filtered_count expected 2; observed 0`) AND/OR SA4/SA7 panics. Restore: `git checkout --`.
    **M3 (F11 — invert attachment threshold direction)**: Edit `crates/ucil-core/src/bonus_selector.rs` `select_bonus_context` body — find `if hit.fused_score >= options.attachment_threshold` and change it to `if hit.fused_score < options.attachment_threshold` (logical inversion: now bonus is attached to LOW-score hits and not to HIGH-score hits). Re-run `cargo test -p ucil-core bonus_selector::test_bonus_context_selection`; MUST FAIL with one of the SA3 (boundary at 0.10 → expected Some, observed None) OR SA4 (below-threshold 0.05 → expected None, observed Some) panic bodies — Rust halts on first failure (most likely SA2 since it asserts top-hit gets bonus, but the inversion ALSO flips SA2: the top hit at 0.50 NO LONGER gets bonus under inverted comparison). Restore: `git checkout --`.
    Each mutation ≤ 3 lines of source change, ≤ 60 seconds wall-time. Pre-stage TWO md5 snapshots (one per file): `md5sum crates/ucil-core/src/context_compiler.rs > /tmp/wo-0088-context-compiler-orig.md5` AND `md5sum crates/ucil-core/src/bonus_selector.rs > /tmp/wo-0088-bonus-selector-orig.md5` BEFORE applying any mutation; confirm restoration via `md5sum -c /tmp/wo-0088-<file>-orig.md5` after each `git checkout --`.
    Spirit-over-literal carve-out per WO-0070/0083/0084/0086 §planner: the executor MAY tighten the targeted SA OR select an alternate-form mutation if the prescribed form (a) compiles but doesn't trip the prescribed SA cleanly, OR (b) the same mutation also flips an earlier SA in assertion order. Document any deviation in the RFR's `## Disclosed deviations` section.

17. **No new Cargo dependency.** `crates/ucil-core/Cargo.toml` MUST NOT change under this WO. Pre-flight: `git diff main -- crates/ucil-core/Cargo.toml | wc -l` returns 0. The `[dev-dependencies]` already includes `tempfile.workspace = true` from WO-0087 (verified at planner-time) but THIS WO's tests do NOT need `tempfile` — they construct in-memory `CrossGroupFusedOutcome` + `Vec<CrossGroupFusedHit>` from literals and use `std::collections::HashSet` + `std::path::PathBuf` + the local `TestBonusContextSource` impl. NO new external crates. The workspace's existing `serde` (already used by `CrossGroupFusedHit`) is sufficient.

18. **§15.2 tracing carve-out** for pure-deterministic helpers (per WO-0067 §planner #2 + WO-0084 §planner pure-deterministic merge-fn precedent + WO-0085 §planner pure-deterministic carve-out for `merge_g7_by_severity`). NEITHER `assemble_response` NOR `select_bonus_context` carries `#[tracing::instrument(...)]` — both are pure-deterministic, no async, no IO, no `tokio::spawn`, no subprocess calls, no logging. The async/IO concerns (KG reads for the production `BonusContextSource` impls; daemon-side response-assembly orchestration) live in follow-up consumer WOs that DO carry `#[tracing::instrument]` annotations on their boundary functions. Document the carve-out inline in each entry-point's rustdoc with a cross-reference: `// §15.2 tracing carve-out applies (pure-deterministic projection) — production impls of [BonusContextSource] in `ucil-daemon` carry `#[tracing::instrument]` at the IO boundary.`.

19. **Errors**: per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Errors — F09 + F11 are pure-deterministic projections that NEVER fail: empty input → empty output, malformed input is structurally unreachable (the `CrossGroupFusedOutcome` shape is type-pinned by WO-0068). NO `Result` / `thiserror::Error` enum needed. NO `anyhow` (binary-only). NO `.unwrap()` / `.expect()` outside `#[cfg(test)]` per .claude/rules/rust-style.md §Errors. Test code MAY use `.expect("<reason>")` per the carve-out (e.g., `result[0].bonus.as_ref().expect("top-hit bonus must be Some")`). If the executor finds an unavoidable `.unwrap()` pattern in production code (e.g., to anchor a mutation contract per WO-0085 W2), proactively cite in the RFR's `## Disclosed deviations` section + add a `# Panics` rustdoc proving unreachability — same shape as WO-0085 `g7.rs:650` precedent.

20. **No `unsafe` blocks. No `#![allow(...)]` at the file or function level (clippy-clean expected).** No `pub use` re-exports beyond the named scope_in #15 surface (NO leaking of internal helpers like a private `partition_by_dedup` step into the public API).

21. **§11 hot-cold tier — DOES NOT APPLY** to this WO (data-only projection over already-fetched outcomes; no cache surfaces; no warm-tier promotion). Both functions run on-demand from the cross-group fusion output; future ADR may explore caching `AssembledResponse` across queries (WO-0085 §technical-debt #5 carry: 'Daemon-side `manifest.transport.env`...' is unrelated). Cite WO-0085 §planner pure-deterministic carve-out as precedent.

22. **Mutation-contract integrity**: when applying M1, M2, OR M3, the mutation MUST surface the regression via the cargo-test panic body (the SA-tagged failure mode named in scope_in #8 / #14). Mutations that produce a compile error OR pass the test silently are NOT acceptable — alternate mutation forms targeting the same load-bearing line ARE acceptable per WO-0070/0083/0084/0086 spirit-over-literal precedent, documented in the RFR's `## Disclosed deviations` section. Per WO-0086 §planner: PREFER constant-flip / value-change patches over comment-out patches whenever the source language permits (M2 is the canonical constant-flip example here).

23. **AC commit-cadence aligned to scope_in actual count per WO-0086 §planner**: this WO is a 2-feature bundle in 2 source files + 1 lib.rs edit. Expected 5-7 commits per DEC-0005 module-coherence: (1) `feat(core): add bonus_selector module with BonusContextSource trait + BonusEntries + select_bonus_context`; (2) `feat(core): extend context_compiler with assemble_response + ResponseMeta + ResponseAssemblyOptions`; (3) `test(core): bonus_selector::test_bonus_context_selection frozen test with TestBonusContextSource impl + 7 SA assertions`; (4) `test(core): context_compiler::test_response_assembly frozen test with 8 SA assertions covering dedup + threshold + token-count + boundary`; (5) `chore(core): wire bonus_selector + context_compiler additions through lib.rs re-exports`; (6) `docs(work-orders): WO-0088 ready-for-review`. The (3)+(4) tests MAY be folded into their respective (1)+(2) feat commits per DEC-0005 module-coherence (then 4 commits total + RFR). Either shape is acceptable. AC30 is reworded as 'feat branch carries 4-7 non-merge commits' to absorb both shapes per WO-0086 §planner alignment.

24. **Carry-forward standing protocol** (43+ WOs deep, WO-0042..WO-0087): coverage-gate.sh sccache RUSTC_WRAPPER workaround — `env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-core --tests --summary-only --json | jq '.data[0].totals.lines.percent'` is the authoritative measurement. Skip the gate-script `coverage-gate.sh ucil-core 85 75` `line=0%` exit per AC25 standing protocol. Note: this WO touches the `ucil-core` package — coverage is measured against `ucil-core` (post-WO baseline ≥85%). Pre-existing standing scope_outs: AC30/AC31 phase-1/phase-2 effectiveness-gate flake (3 open escalations); `scripts/reality-check.sh` pre-existing-stash bug; 'no merge commits introduced on feat' wording.

25. **Verifier dispatch instructions**: MUST run `cargo test -p ucil-core context_compiler::test_response_assembly` AND `cargo test -p ucil-core bonus_selector::test_bonus_context_selection` from a clean session (no `cargo clean` required since the WO is additive in `ucil-core` only — but verifier MAY `cargo clean` if uncertain). MUST NOT set any `UCIL_SKIP_*` env-var (this WO has no skip gate; both tests are fully deterministic, ≤1s wall-time each). Per WO-0086 §verifier 'Pre-flight check' the `which cargo rustc` is sufficient — no node/npx/python needed.

26. **RFR (ready-for-review) MUST include**: (a) summary; (b) M1/M2/M3 mutation contracts in the canonical six-row shape (file, patch, selector, expected SA panic, restore, pre-md5); (c) test-run output for both `test_response_assembly` and `test_bonus_context_selection` (PASS lines + duration); (d) any disclosed deviations from scope_in (per WO-0070/0083/0084/0086 spirit-over-literal pattern); (e) coverage diagnostic (`env -u RUSTC_WRAPPER cargo llvm-cov --package ucil-core --tests --summary-only` line %); (f) pre-md5 snapshot paths confirmed restored via `md5sum -c /tmp/wo-0088-{context-compiler,bonus-selector}-orig.md5` returning OK lines. Mirror the WO-0084 / WO-0085 / WO-0087 RFR Mutation contract section verbatim in shape.

27. **Bundle-shape rationale** (cite in commit body for the first feat commit): 2-feature coordinated bundle per WO-0086 §planner explicit recommendation. P3-W10-F09 + P3-W10-F11 share §6.3 line 660-690 contract; F11 depends on F09 directly per feature-list; bundling avoids cross-feature merge-base churn. Estimated 5-7 commits per DEC-0005 module-coherence + WO-0086 §planner AC-cadence-alignment lesson.

28. **AC #19 'no merge commits introduced on feat' wording** per WO-0070 §planner #4: `git log feat/WO-0088-response-assembly-and-bonus-context ^main --merges` returns empty (NOT the 'feat..main = 0' form).

29. **Word-ban grep** MUST cover only production-side files. The grep `! grep -qiE 'mock|fake|stub' crates/ucil-core/src/context_compiler.rs crates/ucil-core/src/bonus_selector.rs crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs` returns empty before first push. Per WO-0048 line 363 + WO-0069 §executor carve-out, the test body's panic strings (e.g., `(SA1) ...`) are NOT subject to word-ban scrutiny. The `Test` prefix on `TestBonusContextSource` is NOT in the ban list (WO-0083 / WO-0085 precedent).

30. **Frozen test placement** verified PRE-PUSH: `cargo test -p ucil-core context_compiler::test_response_assembly --list 2>&1 | grep -E 'test_response_assembly: test'` shows exactly 1 match AND `cargo test -p ucil-core bonus_selector::test_bonus_context_selection --list 2>&1 | grep -E 'test_bonus_context_selection: test'` shows exactly 1 match. NO `mod tests { ... }` wrapper around either frozen test (per scope_in #7 + #13).

31. **`# Panics` rustdoc requirement** for any `.unwrap()` / `.expect()` shipped in production code (per WO-0085 §planner W2 + RFR-disclosure rule). Both functions are designed to never panic — closure-based filter chains, no array indexing, no Option-unwrapping in production code. If the executor finds an unavoidable `.unwrap()` (e.g., to anchor a mutation contract), the unwrap MUST carry a `# Panics` rustdoc proving unreachability AND the executor MUST cite in the RFR's `## Disclosed deviations` section per WO-0085 lesson.
scope_out: 1. Do NOT wire `assemble_response` / `select_bonus_context` into the daemon's MCP-tool dispatch path OR into any `executor::*` cargo selector — those wirings are the consumer-WO scope (P3-W10-F04 G5 Context query / future response-assembly orchestration WO).

2. Do NOT introduce production `BonusContextSource` impls (`KgBonusContextSource`, `G4BonusContextSource`, `G7BonusContextSource`, `G8BonusContextSource`). Those are deferred to a follow-up production-wiring WO that bundles daemon-startup orchestration — same shape as the G3/G4/G7 production-wiring deferrals (WO-0083 / WO-0085 / WO-0086 §planner). The trait + `TestBonusContextSource` impl in `#[cfg(test)]` are the deliverable.

3. Do NOT introduce a real tokenizer (tiktoken-rs / cl100k_base). The 4-char-per-token heuristic in `hit_token_estimate` is sufficient for the SA6 plausibility-range assertion AND for the WO-0088-scope deliverable per master-plan §6.3 (the master plan does NOT mandate a specific tokenizer for the v0.1.0 release; a future ADR may swap in `tiktoken-rs` for higher-fidelity production budgeting).
4. Do NOT modify the cross-group fusion contract OR `CrossGroupFusedOutcome` shape — F09 consumes the WO-0068 output verbatim. NO `crates/ucil-core/src/cross_group.rs` edits.

5. Do NOT modify `crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs` (G2 RRF surface), `crates/ucil-core/src/tier_merger.rs` (WO-0084 multi-tier merge), `crates/ucil-core/src/ceqp.rs` (WO-0067 reason parsing), `crates/ucil-core/src/incremental.rs` (Salsa surface), `crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs`, `crates/ucil-core/src/otel.rs`, `crates/ucil-core/src/schema_migration.rs`, `crates/ucil-core/src/types.rs` — all out-of-scope (forbidden_paths covers each).
6. Do NOT modify `crates/ucil-daemon/**`, `crates/ucil-treesitter/**`, `crates/ucil-embeddings/**` — F09 + F11 are core-library implementations, not daemon-side wirings.

7. Do NOT alter `scripts/gate/**` or `scripts/flip-feature.sh` (forbidden_paths). NO new `scripts/verify/P3-W10-F09.sh` or `scripts/verify/P3-W10-F11.sh` is needed — both features' acceptance tests are `cargo_test` (not scripts) per `feature-list.json`.

8. Do NOT add new env-vars. The options structs (`ResponseAssemblyOptions`, `BonusSelectionOptions`) carry all knobs via the type system; NO `UCIL_RESPONSE_*` / `UCIL_BONUS_*` env-var is introduced under this WO.
9. Do NOT introduce `unsafe` blocks. The hand-rolled projection logic is safe Rust over `Vec<CrossGroupFusedHit>` + `HashSet<PathBuf>` — no FFI, no transmute, no raw pointers.
10. Do NOT split the WO-0088 deliverable across multiple feat-branches. ONE feat branch (`feat/WO-0088-response-assembly-and-bonus-context`) per DEC-0005 module-coherence.

11. Do NOT add `#[ignore]`, `.skip()`, OR `xfail` to either frozen test. Both are fully deterministic and fast (≤1s wall-time each on the literal in-memory inputs). Per .claude/CLAUDE.md anti-laziness contract.

12. Do NOT add backwards-compat shims for the public `AssembledResponse` / `BonusEntries` / `HitWithBonus` / `BonusSelectionOptions` types — this is the FIRST landing of these types, no consumers exist yet, no compat surface to preserve.

13. Do NOT introduce a separate benchmark file under `crates/ucil-core/benches/`. Benchmark coverage for response-assembly is OUT OF SCOPE (a future ADR may add a `criterion` bench when daemon-side production-wiring lands and the response shape becomes performance-critical).
14. Do NOT introduce or modify any LLM-provider, embedding-provider, OR network-touching surface. Both functions are fully local + deterministic.
15. Do NOT introduce graph-DB or external-service sidecar provisioning. Both functions operate over already-fetched in-memory inputs.
16. Do NOT modify scripts/verify/coverage-gate.sh OR scripts/verify/effectiveness-gate.sh — the standing carry-forwards (43+ WOs deep) remain out-of-scope.
17. Verifier MUST NOT set any `UCIL_SKIP_*` envs (this WO has no skip gate; both tests are purely deterministic).
18. Do NOT add new entries to `feature-list.json` — verifier flips `passes` via `scripts/flip-feature.sh`.
19. Do NOT modify any plugin manifests under `plugins/**` — F09 + F11 are core-library implementations, not plugins.

20. Do NOT modify any tests under `crates/ucil-core/tests/**` — both WO-0088 unit tests live at MODULE ROOT of their respective `.rs` files (NOT as integration tests). The cargo selectors require module-root placement per scope_in #7 / #13.

21. Do NOT introduce additional `#[cfg(test)]` helper structs beyond the named `TestBonusContextSource` (scope_in #12). NO `MockBonusContextSource` / `FakeBonusContextSource` — those names are word-ban-violating per scope_in #29.
22. Do NOT modify `crates/ucil-core/Cargo.toml` `[dependencies]` OR `[dev-dependencies]`. Pre-flight: `git diff main -- crates/ucil-core/Cargo.toml | wc -l` returns 0.

23. Do NOT add `serde::Deserialize` to the new types beyond what `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]` requires. `AssembledResponse` + `BonusEntries` MAY get `serde::Serialize` IF the executor finds a downstream consumer requiring serialization (and ONLY if it doesn't introduce a new dep — `serde` is already a workspace dep). Default: NO serde derives unless explicitly required by a consumer (none in this WO).

24. Do NOT introduce trait objects (`Box<dyn BonusContextSource>` / `Arc<dyn BonusContextSource>`) in the public API. `select_bonus_context<S: BonusContextSource>` is generic per scope_in #11; trait-object storage (e.g., `Arc<Vec<Arc<dyn BonusContextSource>>>` like WO-0083 `with_g4_sources`) is the consumer-WO concern (when daemon-side multiple-source aggregation lands).
